=== [Skill Rings] ===
Skill Ring HTML ConvertTime 0.256 sec. Weapons/Armor (Series) Sword (PA) Wired Lance (PA) Partisan (PA) Twin Dagger (PA) Double Saber (PA) Knuckle (PA) Katana (PA) Dual Blade (PA) Assault Rifle (PA) Launcher (PA) Twin Machine Gun (PA) Bullet Bow (PA) Gun Slash (PA) Rod (AC) Talis (PA) Wand (PA) Jet Boots (PA) Tact (PA) Armor Skill Ring Technique Fire Attribute Ice Attribute Lightning Attribute Wind Attribute Light Attribute Dark Attribute Complex Attribute Equipment Enhancement/Technique/PA Customization Item Enhancement/Limit Break Multi Weapon Special Abilities (Comparison Table) EX Special Abilities Potential Abilities Preset Abilities Tech Arts Customization Table of Contents Overview Enhancement List Type 1 Type 1/Killing Rise Type 1/Swish Rise Type 1/HP Key Prize Type 1/Under PP Rise Type 1/Patient Rise Type 1/Defeat Rise Type 1/Photon Blast Rise Type 1/Count Rise Type 1/Simple Attack Type 2 Type 2/Swish Follow Type 2/Over HP Follow Type 2/Under PP Follow Type 2/Patient Follow Type 2/Photon Blast Follow Type 2/Count Follow Type 2/Simple Guard Digression Comments Overview Implemented October 8, 2025 Equipment that is classified as armor that becomes effective by meeting the "described conditions", such as EX special abilities. There are ``Type 1'' that mainly reinforces firepower, and ``Type 2'' that reinforces defense and efficiency, and one of each type can be equipped from the ``Armor'' window. Rings can only be attached, removed, and changed within the ARCS base. The name is "Type ◯/(Skill Ring Name)". Unless otherwise noted, it can be obtained through limited-time quests and limited-time emergency quests after the implementation date. Unlike the recent drop policy, it seems that once implemented, it will not be dropped in all content distributed in the future. A few types are picked up for each quest, and others do not drop during that quest. ▼ Addition/Change History 2026/04/08 Added the following skill rings Type 1/Count Rise Type 2/Count Follow 2026/04/01 Added the following skill rings Type 1/Photon Blast Rise Type 1/Defeat Rise Type 2/Photon Blast Follow 2025/10/22 Update Added the following skill rings Type 1/Under PP Rise Type 1/Patient Rise Type 2/Under PP Follow Type 2/Patient Follow 2025/10/08 Update Added the following skill rings Type 1/Swish Rise Type 1/HP Key Prize Type 1/Killing Rise Type 2/Swish Follow Type 2/Over HP Follow Type 2/Simple Guard ▲ ▼ Strengthen You can "strengthen skill rings" using skill rings with the same name as materials in the item lab. The reinforcement value of the material does not affect the number required for reinforcement. If the reinforcement value of the material is higher than the reinforcement target, it cannot be used as a material. Based on the above conditions, it is recommended that the material be unreinforced. Enhancement value +1 +2 +3 +4 +5 Cumulative material with the same name 1 1 2 2 3 9 Enhancement cost 100,000 500,000 1,000,000 2,000,000 3,000,000 6,600,000 Success rate 100% - For now, just remember that you will need a total of 10 rings to fully strengthen (1 for equipment, 9 for materials). There is a ``Skill Ring Enhancement Substitute Material'' as an auxiliary item. It can be used in place of the material if the reinforcement value is less than or equal to the substitute material. Like other substitute materials-based auxiliary items, when used, you can increase the reinforcement value of the base item by one without using the original reinforcement material. Even if two or more materials are required, one substitute material can be substituted. Cannot be listed in My Shop Skill Ring Enhancement Substitute Material/+0 AC Shop 700AC (Can be purchased once every 4 weeks) Skill Ring Enhancement Substitute Material/+3 AC Scratch Frequency Bonus (110 times) Substitute Material +0 can only be used to strengthen an unenhanced ring to +1. Substitute material +3 can be used to strengthen a ring with an enhancement value of 0 to 3, and can be used to strengthen a ring with an enhancement value of +3 to increase it to +4. ▲ ▼ List Rare 1/Killing Rise Power against enemies other than bosses +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ 4.00 )%/ When defeating enemies around yourself For 30 seconds, power +1.00 (1.50/2.00/2.50/2.75/ 3.00 )% When Rise Power against boss enemies +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ 4.00 )% / Power +1.00 (1.50/2.00/2.50/2.75/ 3.00 )% for 20 seconds when attacks are nullified by steps, weapon actions, etc. Power increases by 3.02%/4.04%/5.06%/6.09%/6.6%/7.12% when additional effects are activated.In addition to evasion actions, weapon actions, and successful defense with each guard point, it is also activated when Adras Perfection is used. Since the condition is to ``nullify the attack'' through the above actions, it will not be activated if you slip through due to the invincibility state of PB or some skill activations. The power increase activated when "attack is nullified" is also valid for enemies other than bosses. ▲ ▼ Type 1/HP Keep Prize The more HP remaining, the more power increases, maximum +3.00 (4.00/5.00/6.00/6.50/ 7.00 )%/ Recovers 1.00 (2.00/3.00/4.00/4.50/ 5.00 )% of HP every 20 seconds.The indicated value will be applied to the power increase effect while HP is 100%. When the remaining HP is 90% or more and less than 100%, the effect will decrease to 90%. After that, the effect will decrease by 10% (0.3/0.4/0.5/0.6/0.65/0.7%) each time the remaining HP decreases by 10%, such as less than 90%, less than 80%, etc., and the power increase effect will be disabled while the remaining HP is less than 10%. Due to this structure, it is more appropriate to say ``the less HP remaining, the lower the power'' than to say ``the more HP remaining, the more power increases.'' Although the activation conditions are strict, there is no specification of the effect target. HP recovery effect does not activate when HP is 100%. Although it says ``Recover every 20 seconds,'' the count does not start until HP decreases to less than 100%, and in reality, the effect is ``When HP decreases, it will recover by the indicated percentage every 20 seconds until it is fully recovered.'' Therefore, it is not compatible with EX turnover alone. It goes well with configurations that make it easy to keep HP 100%. On the other hand, configurations that reduce HP (see Type 1/Patient Rise) are not recommended. Te, which has recovery methods other than Resta sign, Br, which can recover passively, Wa, which recovers when hit and can save on Resta sign consumption, and long-range weapons in multiplayer where it is difficult to get hit. The effectiveness of EX Try Shield (+ EX Avoid Onslaught composition with high affinity with it) Type 2/Patient Follow depends on the maximum HP and the degree of damage taken, but it is effective if it can take down one shot. ▲ ▼ Type 1/Under PP Rise PP consumption increase +20%, PA and technique power +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ 4.00 )% Power +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ when PP is below 45 (50/55/60/65/ 70 )% 4.00 )% When additional effects are activated, power +4.04%/5.06%/6.09%/7.12%/7.64%/8.16% Please note that among the type 1 rings implemented as of October 2025, this is the only type 1 ring that has an effect limited to "PA and technique". Classes for which PA/technique power is important (classes that are less dependent on counters and active skills) include Gu, Ra, and Hu (sword/pulch). EX special abilities with similar activation conditions include EX Consume PP Onslaught and EX Shortage PP Save. ▲ ▼ Type 1/Patient Rise Recovers 7 (9/11/13/14/ 15 )% PP when HP decreases, recast for 30 seconds Power +3.00 (4.00/5.00/6.00/6.50/ 7.00 )% for 30 seconds A ring that is almost predicated on using a method to actively reduce HP. EX Shortage PP Burn Up (Adds burn when 100 PP is consumed, damage occurs every 2 seconds) EX Risky Stance (HP decreases with a 10% chance of hitting a hit/recast for 10 seconds) EX Keep Balance (HP decreases when HP is 50% or higher when hitting a hit. In other words, a separate regular recovery method is required) Regular HP recovery methods that activate even at 100% HP: Type 2/Swish Follow (10 seconds recast), EX Pain Rouse (20 seconds recast), EX Avoid HP Gain (10 seconds recast) Periodic HP recovery methods that do not activate at 100% HP: Potential abilities of some weapons (Huvert series/10 seconds recast). Type 1/HP Keep Prize cannot be used in conjunction with the same category in the first place Fo Skill PP Convert (Recast 150 seconds/Stock 2 times) Recast is only involved in the PP recovery part, and if you receive damage during the 30 second duration of power increase, the duration will return to 30 seconds each time. ▲ ▼ Type 1/Defeat Rise Power +3.00 (4.00/5.00/6.00/6.50/ 7.00 )% / Down value given to enemies +2 (4/6/8/9/ 10 )% / Power +1% for 20 seconds when enemies are down around you Power increases by 4.03%/5.04%/6.05%/7.06%/7.56%/8.07% when the additional effect is activated.Although it is a ring that strengthens the power when it is knocked down, the constant effect alone is very good at up to 7%, probably because there are some enemies that cannot be knocked down in the first place. Unless you can maintain the additional effects of other rings with some degree of stability, this one is strong enough. The extent to which a "down" judgment is included needs to be verified. It also has the rare effect of increasing the down value of all attacks unconditionally. There are countless ways to increase power, but there are very few ways to increase down value. Specific strengthening methods include Bo skills Defeat Amplify, Physical Decline, Elemental Decline, EX Gradual Pressing, Talis PA perforate chute (customization: type 2) etc. However, even if you vaguely increase the down value, there are not many cases where the number of downs increases just by making downs happen faster, and the additional power when downed is +1%, which is not a large number, so the effect is weak. In battles against raid bosses, if you earn too much down value, the BREAK down will be faster, and there is a possibility that you will not be able to use Photon Blast in time. It shows its true value in time attack, where the strategy is established and the down value is often insufficient. ▲ ▼ Type 1/Photon Blast Rise Power +3.00(3.50/4.00/4.50/4.75/ 5.00 )%/ Power +1.00(1.50/2.00/2.50/2.75/ 3.00 )% for 90 seconds after Photon Blast activation. Power increases by 4.03%/5.05%/6.08%/7.11%/7.63%/8.15% when the additional effect is activated.The duration of the additional effect is long and the amount of reinforcement is excellent, but until the first Photon Blast is activated, only the constant effect is obtained. After that, even if you prepare recast reductions such as add-on skills, if you can't activate Photon Blast every time you recast, the reinforcement will be interrupted, so it's a little difficult to handle. For Techters who have multiple skills that increase the amount of gauge accumulated and have the advantage of shooting photon blasts even when down. Is it easy to use if you know that Blast Charge Signs can be obtained early or regularly through limited time quests? ▲ ▼ Type 1/Count Rise Power +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ 4.00 )% / When the enemy is hit 30 times, power +2.00 (2.50/3.00/3.50/3.75/ 4.00 )% for 20 seconds. Power increases by 4.04%/5.06%/6.09%/7.12%/7.64%/8.16% when additional effects are activated.If you hit 30 times while additional effects are active, the duration returns to 20 seconds. It depends on the weapon type, but if you can keep attacking, you can generally maintain it. However, in reality, there may be small periods of time when you cannot attack due to movement or movies, so the actual number of hits required per hour is more severe than the face value. It's surprisingly difficult to maintain stability unless you're dealing with an enemy that doesn't move, such as when you're down. Especially in high-difficulty/emergency quests, there are many situations where you can counter continuously, and most weapon types have counters with low hits and high power, so the number of hits decreases and it becomes difficult to maintain. In addition, some weapon types such as photon blasts and active skills that have a low number of hits may run out if the timing is wrong. As long as you can maintain the additional effects, the amount of reinforcement is excellent, and the condition is simple, just attack, but it can be said that it is a ring for slightly advanced users who can choose the type of weapon and opponent. Especially compatible with Gunner. Originally, due to Chain Trigger, it was based on 100 to 150 hits per 20 seconds instead of 30, so it can be maintained without thinking too much. Blitz style has a high number of hits with Decay Bullet when it is weak and trigger attacks, which is a powerful aid to maintaining it. ▲ ▼ Type 1/Simple Attack Power +3% Cannot be listed in My Shop A skill ring that can be obtained as a clear reward (enhancement value +5) for the [Achieved class level 65] task in the 2026/06/03 update. Unless the opponent is at a significantly lower level, you cannot expect continuous activation without recovery. There is no damage reduction effect on maximum HP percentage damage such as Stia's volcanic bomb. Without reinforcement, the damage reduction performance is almost a bonus. If you are only looking for the robust effect, you can use it without reinforcement. Since you will be able to push through your actions regardless of the opponent's attack, you will be able to use it in the same way as EX Try Shield. Although it is inferior to Tri-Shield in that the damage itself is taken, it is superior to Tri-Shield in that it can be activated repeatedly without recasting as long as it recovers, and can be activated against attacks that cannot be blocked. It loses its effectiveness if its HP cannot be maintained at 95% or above, so it is weak against situations where it is hit continuously at high speed, but some believe that it is effective against continuous attacks that can be dealt with as long as it can overcome even the first hit. ▲ ▼ Type 2 / Under PP Follow When PP is below 45 (50/55/60/65/ 70 )%, PP recovery amount when attacking +7 (10/13/16/18/ 20 )% / When PP is below 20%, 30% PP recovery, recast 90 (80/70/60/50/ 45 ) seconds Type 1 / Under PP Rise A ring that can offset the disadvantages of It is easy to adjust PP because it increases the amount of PP recovered during attacks rather than the amount of natural recovery. As the name suggests, it is easy to use for simply following PP consumption. Especially around fighters that consume a lot of PP. ▲ ▼ Type 2 / Patient Follow Recovers 4 (8/12/16/18/ 20 )% HP when HP decreases, recast 30 seconds Damage received -2 ​​(5/8/11/13/ 15 )% for 10 seconds A ring that activates under similar conditions as Type 1 / Patient Rise. The HP recovery effect is activated the moment you are hit. (Same as Wa Skill Old Friends Rescue) Once activated, it enters a recast period and will not activate during that time. Recovery occurs immediately after HP decreases, so it is not possible to prevent inability to fight due to damage that exceeds the current HP. The damage reduction effect has a short duration (10 seconds). However, recasting only affects the HP recovery part, and if you receive damage during the 10 second duration of the damage reduction effect, the duration will return to 10 seconds each time. Unless you also use a method that reduces HP, the damage reduction effect will not apply to the first damage (the damage reduction effect is activated when HP decreases, and is applied to damage received while the effect lasts). For methods of actively reducing HP, see the Type 1/Patient Rise section. All you need to do is take damage, so it's not bad with EX Risky Stance, which only takes 1 damage, but the HP reduction rate is 10% per hit, which is difficult to stabilize depending on the weapon. Also, in order to take advantage of the HP recovery effect, it is necessary to continue fighting without recovering to some extent. Regarding the combination of EX Shortage PP Burnup and EX Hysterical Strength, the burn damage interval is short at 2 seconds and can be activated stably, but even at +5, the ring alone cannot compensate for the loss of HP. (Burn Lv5 is 1.5%/2 seconds = 22.5%/30 seconds) However, it will be much easier to handle EX effects. It can also be activated by self-inflicted acts such as PP conversion. ▲ ▼ Type 2 / Photon Blast Follow Damage taken -1.00 (2.00/3.00/4.00/4.50/ 5.00 )% / After photon blast is activated, photon blast gauge increase +7 (10/13/16/18/ 20 )% for 90 seconds when attacking A ring that not only reduces damage taken, but also makes it easier to accumulate photon blasts from the second time onwards. Until the first photon blast is activated, it only has a damage reduction effect. It is also a rare type 2 ring that has an effect on attack by itself. ▲ ▼ Type 2/Count Follow When attacks hit the enemy 30 times, recovers HP by 1.00 (2.00/3.00/4.00/4.50/ 5.00 )%, recovers PP by 4 (8/12/16/18/ 20 )%, recast for 10 seconds The properties of the recovery effect and recast are similar to Type 2/Swish Follow, but The advantage of this is that it activates just by attacking, regardless of the opponent's actions. The HP recovery effect is activated even when HP is fully recovered. There is no time limit for hitting 30 attacks, and the count will not be reset under any conditions other than when the effect is activated. For example, even if you wait for 1 minute after attacking 20 times, the recovery effect will be activated after the next 10 attacks. The number of hits made during the recast will not be counted at all, and you will need to make another 30 hits after 10 seconds have passed. Therefore, the actual activation interval is the recast time plus the time it takes to hit 30 times. When combined with EX Turnover, which has an effect duration of 20 seconds, the effect ends if you do not hit 30 times within 10 seconds after recasting. It is relatively easy to use for Gunners and Fighters who require a lot of moves, but it is difficult to constantly activate it with just the ring effect for weapon types that do not earn many moves. ▲ ▼ Type 2/Simple Guard -5% damage taken Cannot be listed in My Shop A skill ring that can be obtained as a commemorative present (reinforcement value +5). ▲ ▼ Digression The following is advance information *1 *2 given by the development team in a media interview. As an image, it will be something like "Skill Ring" from "PSO2". As the "third equipment" after weapons and armor, it will be possible to equip two slots at the time of distribution. In "PSO2", it was positioned as an external class skill that functions in a specific class, but in "NGS" it is planned to have an effect similar to an EX special ability. Even if the effect is the same, there are variations that can be activated in a way that suits your play style, such as ``activate when evading,'' ``activate when PP is consumed,'' and ``activate when HP is maintained at a certain level.'' Skill rings in PSO2 were made by collecting gathering materials, but in NGS they are dropped directly as rings. The probability is that it will require a certain amount of play, but since it can be used immediately after obtaining it, it is easy to test its effects. If you end up wearing the same ring, you can use it to strengthen the ring so it doesn't go to waste. Future additions are being considered based on user reactions, acquisition status, usage status, etc. ▲ ▼ Comments Displaying the latest 20 comments. === [Equipment Enhancement] ===

=== [Item Enhancement and Limit Breaking] ===
Item enhancement/Limit Break HTML ConvertTime 0.354 sec. Weapons/Armor (Series) Sword (PA) Wired Lance (PA) Partisan (PA) Twin Dagger (PA) Double Saber (PA) Knuckle (PA) Katana (PA) Dual Blade (PA) Assault Rifle (PA) Launcher (PA) Twin Machine Gun (PA) Bullet Bow (PA) ) Gun Slash (PA) Rod (AC) Talis (PA) Wand (PA) Jet Boots (PA) Tact (PA) Armor Skill Ring Technique Fire Attribute Ice Attribute Lightning Attribute Wind Attribute Light Attribute Dark Attribute Complex Attribute Equipment Enhancement/Technique/PA Customization Item Enhancement/Limit Breaking Multi Weapon Special Abilities (Comparison Table) EX Special Abilities Potential Abilities Preset Abilities Tech Arts Customization Table of Contents Overview Item enhancement Added value of weapon attack power and armor defense power due to enhancement Required enhancement EXP table Limit breakthrough Materials and N-Meseta required to release the upper limit Materials Auxiliary items Increased success rate of enhancement/discount period for limit breakthrough cost About enhancement methods with high cost performance Enhancement EXP obtained when using enhanced equipment as material Creation of enhancement materials using event exchange items Comments Overview Central City, Rithem City, Kvaris Camp, Stear Camp You can strengthen weapons and armor with ``Item Enhancement'' in the Item Lab. When enhanced, enhancement EXP is accumulated in the base item, and when a certain amount of enhancement EXP is reached, the enhancement value increases. To strengthen it, you need N-Grinder, N-Meseta, another weapon for weapons, and another armor for armor. Weapons and armor to be strengthened are called "base items", and other weapons and armor necessary for strengthening are called "material items". The upper limit of the reinforcement value in the initial state varies depending on the rarity of the base item. The current upper limit is written as "(MAX10)" in the "Enhancement value" column of item details tab 2. The upper limit can be expanded by "Limit Break" in the Item Lab, but the final enhancement limit may vary depending on the rarity. To break the limit, you need N-Meseta, ore up to +40, Arms Refiner after +50, and Arms Refiner II after +70 for ★1 to ★6 and +60 for ★7 or higher. Materials such as ores used to break through the limits are treated as "costs" and are affected by cost discounts. Breaking the limit itself can be done regardless of the current reinforcement value. As the reinforcement value increases, the number of special abilities (number of slots) that can be attached increases. Reinforcement value Number of slots Unreinforced ~ +19 2 +20 ~ +39 3 +40 ~ +49 4 +50 ~ +60 5 +61 ~ +80 6 +81 ~ +99 7 +100 8 No failure in item enhancement or limit breaking. Equipment from the old PSO2 cannot be enhanced on the NGS side, nor can it be turned into a material item. ▼ Change History 2025/06/04 Update The enhancement limit (upper limit) for Limit Break has been changed from 90 to 100. 2024/12/04 Update Changed so that up to 10 material items can be used at a time for item enhancement (previously it was 5 at a time) 2024/06/05 Update The enhancement limit (upper limit) for Limit Break has been changed from 80 to 90. 2023/12/06 Update The enhancement limit (upper limit) for Limit Break has been changed from 70 to 80. 2023/06/14 The enhancement limit (upper limit) for Limit Break has been changed from 60 to 70. 2023/01/25 The enhancement limit (upper limit) for Limit Break for rarity★7~8 equipment has been changed from 50 to 60. 2022/10/05 Update The enhancement limit (upper limit) for breaking the limit for rarity ★ 1 to 6 equipment has been changed from 50 to 60. 2022/02/09 Update The enhancement limit (upper limit) for breaking the limit has been changed from 40 to 50. ▲ ▼ Item enhancement Up to 10 material items can be used at a time. One N-Grinder is required for each material item. Cost per material item Base item Rarity Enhancement cost ★14 ★13 7,500 ★12 5,900 ★11 5,700 ★10 5,500 ★9 5,200 ★8 5,000 ★7 4,700 ★6 4,500 ★5 4,200 ★4 4,000 ★3 3,000 ★2 2,000 ★1 1,000 Enhancement EXP obtained per material item Material item Rarity Enhancement EXP obtained ★14 ★13 1,500 ★12 1,400 ★11 1,300 ★10 1,200 ★9 1,100 ★8 1,000 ★7 900 ★6 800 ★5 700 ★4 600 ★3 350 ★2 200 ★1 100 If the material item has the following special abilities, you can get additional reinforcement EXP Special abilities Enhancement EXP Esperio I 400 Esperio II 450 Hyperio 5,000 Hyperio II 1,000,000 Endymio 100,000 Enhancement experience points +500,000 500,000 Astraio 5,000,000 Enhancement EXP obtained from the total of items (EXP of the item itself + EXP from special abilities) obtained from Battle Deer and AC Scratch Item Enhancement EXP obtained Silver Prim Sword +20 Silva Prim Armor +20 2,850 Gold Prim Sword +20 Goldo Prim Armor +20 4,550 Goldo Prim Sword +30 Goldo Prim Armor +30 8,300 N-EXP Weapon +20 N-EXP Armor +20 7,450 Goldo Prim Sword II Goldo Prim Armor II 100,900 N-EXP Weapon II N-EXP Armor II 1,000,200 If a material item is enhanced, half of the enhancement EXP accumulated in the material item will be obtained as additional enhancement EXP. If the material item has the same name as the base item, the enhanced EXP obtained will be multiplied by 1.5 as a bonus with the same name. Enhancement EXP added by special abilities is also subject to bonuses with the same name, so it is more efficient to add special abilities to material items with the same name before using them. If you achieve a "great success", the enhanced EXP you gain will be doubled. The probability of success is 10%. You can increase the probability with auxiliary items. The final enhanced EXP obtained is calculated using the following formula. Obtained enhancement EXP = (EXP from material item + EXP accumulated in material item ÷ 2 + EXP from special ability) x Bonus with the same name x Great success multiplier ▲ ▼ Additional value of weapon attack power and armor defense power due to enhancement A fixed value set for each rarity and enhancement value of the base item is added. Ordinal Tower 5 N-★4 Limit Break Substitute Material/Limit Value 40 When breaking the limit of an item, the required material item to increase the enhancement limit value from 30 to 40 for items with rarity ★4 or lower is no longer required. Ordinal Tower 5 N-★4 Limit Break Substitute Material/Limit Value 50 When breaking an item's limit, the required material item to increase the enhancement limit value from 40 to 50 for items with rarity ★4 or lower is no longer required. "PSO2" 9th ANNIVERSARY" 2nd reward period ▲ ▼ About the cost-effective reinforcement method Currently, Gold Prim Sword II, Basically, use Gold Prim Armor II or C/Endimio. These can be obtained from boss enemies after level 70, exchange shops, etc. You can trade them in My Shop, so even if you can't obtain them on your own, it's cheaper to buy them than to invest in a large amount of equipment with low experience points. If you are aiming to strengthen it to the upper limit, even if you use Gordo II, the strengthening cost alone will cost you a considerable amount of meseta. In that case, you can reduce the cost of strengthening by following the steps below and strengthening all at once to the upper limit. Open the upper limit of the equipment you want to strengthen with "Breakthrough" Prepare multiple pieces of equipment that have been strengthened to "+70" or higher (we recommend purchasing strengthened obsolete items from My Shop or creating them using event exchange items below) Prepare "N-Strengthening Great Success Rate +100%" (you can get it as a special scratch prize) Insert the reinforced equipment into the equipment you want to strengthen and use the 100% reinforcement success rate to strengthen it. ▲ ▼ Enhancement EXP obtained when using enhanced equipment as material ★ Material name Enhancement EXP obtained 11 "Eredym" series +60 (weapon) 74,800 7 Gordo II 100,900 7 Gordo II +60 150,900 7 Gordo II +80 9,850,900 7 Gordo II +90 14,700,900 7 Gordo II +100 19,550,900 4 Qualde Armor +80 9,435,600 6 Sestat Armor +80 9,645,800 7 Ajax Armor +80 9,750,900 *The value of Gordo II includes Endimio (+100,000) *The cost to add Endimio to other equipment is 100 Meseta. ▲ ▼ Creating reinforcement materials using event exchange items In the seasonal event, "Gold Prim Sword II +60 that has exceeded its limit" can be exchanged. During the Enhancement Great Success Rate +90% period, if you combine the Great Success Bonus and the bonus with the same name, you can increase the Enhancement EXP like a snowball. (0.5*2*1.5=1.5) Significant savings can be made in reinforcement costs. We recommend locking all items together to prevent accidental recycling. For the time being, if you have "N-Reinforcement Great Success Rate +100%" left over, you can create it regardless of the period. Exchange Gordo Prim Sword II +60, which has exceeded the limit, at the exchange shop. Wait for the period of Enhancement Great Success Rate +90% (100% together with the base value) (lock and store it until the period). Prepare an unenhanced Gold Prim Sword II. (C/Endimio capsule cannot receive the bonus of the same name, so exchange it for C/Endimio at the enhancement lab.) Inject only one Goldo Prim Sword II to strengthen it. Input only one of the items strengthened in 4. into another Goldo Prim Sword II +60 as a material. Add another Gold Prim Sword II +60 to 5. Repeat this until it reaches +80 to 100. (If you do it correctly, the reinforcement value will progress as +61 → +63 → +65 → +68 → +73 → +80 → +90 → +100.) If you make everything +100, depending on the rarity of the reinforcement target, the experience points may overflow, so if you don't like that, use the required reinforcement EXP and the acquired reinforcement EXP. It is a good idea to adjust the amount to be created by stopping at +80 or +90 with reference to. If you want to save the replacement Gold Prim Sword II +60 and create more reinforcement materials, increase the number of unreinforced Gold Prim Sword II used in step 4. When strengthening to +90, the required number of unenhanced Gold Prim Sword II (hereinafter referred to as materials) and the meseta consumed during reinforcement are as follows for the consumption of Gold Prim Sword II +60 (hereinafter referred to as replacement item). The reinforcement cost is the cost per completed product, and the number of completed products is assumed to be 80 replacement products. Replacement item Quantity consumed Unreinforced material Quantity consumed Reinforcement cost (Meseta) Reinforcement cost + material Assumed to be 1000 Meseta Reinforcement cost + Material Assumed to be 3000 Meseta Finished product Quantity 1 97 455,900 552,900 746,900 80 2 64 305,500 369,500 499,500 40 3 42 202,100 244,100 328,100 26 4 27 136,300 163,300 217,300 20 5 17 94,000 111,000 145,000 16 6 11 70,500 81,500 103,500 13 7 6 51,700 57,700 69,700 11 8 3 42,300 45,300 51,300 10 9 1 37,600 38,600 40,600 8 10 0 37,600 37,600 37,600 8 Super Mugen Festival Since 2025, Gold Prim Armor II has been added as an exchange item, so armor reinforcement materials can be created using the same procedure. ▼ When using replacement armor other than Gold Prim Armor II as material In the seasonal event (as of Super Genesis Festival ’24), you can exchange 6 pieces each of various armor such as “Qualde Armor +60,” “Sestat Armor +60,” and “Ajax Armor +60,” which have already exceeded the limit to +90. (If short-term seasonal events are held consecutively, such as Christmas → New Year → Winter, there will be three each.) The following is an example from Ajax Armor. Exchange 6 Ajax Armor +60 that has exceeded the limit Prepare 18 C/Endimio capsules Wait for the period of 90% reinforcement success rate (100% with base value) (lock and store until the period) Inject 18 C/Endimio into A's Ajax Armor +60 and strengthen it. Inject A (Ajax Armor +63) strengthened in (4) into B's Ajax Armor +60 as a material. Inject B (Ajax Armor +65) strengthened in (5) into another Ajax Armor +60 as a material. Add the one strengthened in (6) to another Ajax Armor +60, and so on until it reaches +80~90 (If done correctly, the reinforcement value will progress as +63 → +65 → +68 → +73 → +80 → +90) By changing C/Endimio in step (5) to "Unenhanced Ajax Armor with Endymio", the number of inputs = consumption meseta can be reduced, but it takes time to prepare materials and give special abilities. Also, by using refiners, you can save on event replacement items. During the same name synthesis, parts of the reinforcement value that do not exceed 70 can be substituted with armor of the same name that has exceeded the upper limit of 70, and between 70 and 80 can be replaced with armor of the same name that has exceeded the upper limit of 80. It is assumed that the limit breakthrough cost is discounted, but the cost is slightly lower than creating +90 without using the reinforcement of the same name. ▼ Steps to strengthen the Gold Prim Sword II exchanged at the event to +100 *Click on the image to enlarge ▲ ▼ Comments Displaying the latest 20 items. Weapons/Armor (Series) Sword (PA) Wired Lance (PA) Partisan (PA) Twin Dagger (PA) Double Saber (PA) Knuckle (PA) Katana (PA) Dual Blade (PA) Assault Rifle (PA) Launcher (PA) Twin Machine Gun (PA) Bullet Bow (PA) Gun Slash (PA) Rod (AC) Talis (PA) Wand (PA) Jet Boots (PA) Tact (PA) Armor Skill Ring Technique Fire Attribute Ice Attribute Lightning Attribute Wind Attribute Light Attribute Dark Attribute Complex Attribute Equipment Enhancement/Technique/PA Customization Item Enhancement/Limit Break Multi Weapon Special Abilities (Comparison Table) EX Special Abilities Potential Abilities Preset Abilities Tech Arts Customization Table of Contents Overview List Comments Overview Can be equipped from Main Menu → Equipment/Sub Palette → Armor Equipment. There are three equipment slots: Slot1, 2, and 3. There are no restrictions on equipment depending on class. Like weapons, it is possible to enhance them and add special abilities. Preset abilities specific to armor are also granted. See Damage Calculation for the effect of each parameter on damage. The distinction between the rear, arms, and legs of the old PSO2 is gone, and they are all treated like subunits. It is no longer reflected in appearance (always in stealth state). Weapons/Armor (Series) Sword (PA) Wired Lance (PA) Partisan (PA) Twin Dagger (PA) Double Saber (PA) Knuckle (PA) Katana (PA) Dual Blade (PA) Assault Rifle (PA) Launcher (PA) Twin Machine Gun (PA) Bullet Bow (PA) Gun Slash (PA) Rod (AC) Talis (PA) Wand (PA) Jet Boots (PA) Tact (PA) Armor Skill Ring Technique Fire Attribute Ice Attribute Lightning Attribute Wind Attribute Light Attribute Dark Attribute Complex Attribute Equipment Enhancement/Technique/PA Customization Item Enhancement/Limit Break Multi Weapon Special Abilities (Comparison Table) EX Special Abilities Potential Abilities Preset Abilities Tech Arts Customization Table of Contents Overview List Comment Overview One of the weapons that Tector can equip. It is sometimes written as "short cane". For basic behavior, photon blast, PA, etc., see Wand/Action/PA. In addition to long-range attacks using techniques, powerful blow attacks are possible at close range. With the wand in NGS, all attacks, including normal attacks, PA, legal explosions, and techniques, are treated as legal weapon attacks. To increase its power, add special abilities that increase the power of legal weapons (such as Technique I). It is not an increase in the power of striking weapons (such as Power I). ▲ ▼ List If you would like to attach an icon image below, please use "48x48px" "Item name.png". For more information, please see the WIKI Editors' Meeting. Please refer to the Weapons/Armor/Series or Sword page for a comparison of the attack power of each series' reinforcement values. Technique HTML ConvertTime 0.149 sec. Weapons/Armor (Series) Sword (PA) Wired Lance (PA) Partisan (PA) Twin Dagger (PA) Double Saber (PA) Knuckle (PA) Katana (PA) Dual Blade (PA) Assault Rifle (PA) Launcher (PA) Twin Machine Gun (PA) Bullet Bow (PA) Gun Slash (PA) Rod (AC) Talis (PA) Wand (PA) Jet Boots (PA) Tact (PA) Armor Skill Ring Technique Fire Attribute Ice Attribute Lightning Attribute Wind Attribute Light Attribute Dark Attribute Complex Attribute Equipment Enhancement/Technique/PA Customization Item Enhancement/Limit Break Multi Weapon Special Abilities (Comparison Table) EX Special Abilities Potential Abilities Preset Abilities Tech Arts Customization Table of Contents Overview Complex Attribute Technique List of Techniques Comments Overview Techniques are so-called magic. The setting of the Phantasy Star series is that ``magic'' requires innate talent, and ``technique'' is the reproduction of magic using science and technology. Techniques have six attributes: fire, ice, lightning, wind, light, and darkness, and each technique behaves very differently. Note that the classes and weapon types that can be used with complex attribute techniques are different from regular techniques, and it is also necessary to learn specific class skills. To use a technique (excluding complex attribute techniques described below), you must set a class that can use the technique as your main class or subclass, and equip a weapon type that can use the technique. Usable Classes Force Tector Bouncer Usable Weapons Rod Talis Wand Jet Boots Technique attacks have the following characteristics. By charging (holding down the button), the power and behavior change. Attacks have attributes (weapon attacks are non-attribute, except for some weapons with attributes). Attacks with an attribute that matches the enemy's weak point attribute will increase damage. By attacking an enemy with a weak point attribute technique, you can lower the attribute. ▲ ▼ Mixed Attribute Techniques Combined Attribute Techniques can only be used with Force and Techter weapons. It is also necessary to learn the class skill "Unite Technique Fo" or "Unite Technique Te". Usable Class: Force Tector Usable Weapon Type: Rod Talis Wand Unlike normal techniques, it consumes a "composite attribute technique gauge" instead of PP. There is no damage increase due to matching charge or weak point attributes, and class skills that target techniques are not applied. See Techniques/Complex Attributes for details. ▲ ▼ Technique List Name Power Consumed PP Text Foie 300 15 Fire attribute technique. Shoots a fireball in front of you. Charging changes the attack behavior. Guy Foie 430 20 Fire attribute technique. Generates a vortex of flame around yourself. Charging changes the attack behavior. Bata 300 16 Ice attribute technique. Fires an ice bullet in front of you. Power increases by charging. Gi Baata 310 21 Ice attribute technique. Generates an ice blade in front of you. Power increases by charging. Sonde 300 18 Lightning attribute technique. Drops lightning in front of you. Power increases by charging. Gi Zonde 330 23 Lightning attribute technique. Releases a spherical energy body into the air, intermittently delivering lightning strikes to single enemies within range. Charging changes the attack behavior. Zan 290 17 Wind attribute technique. Fires a blade of wind in front of you. Charging changes the attack behavior. Gui Zan 270 20 Wind attribute technique. Creates a tornado in front of you. Power increases by charging. Granz 310 18 Light attribute technique. Drops a sword of light in front of you. Charging changes the attack behavior. Gi Grants 270 21 Light attribute technique. Clears the area around you with a sword of light. Charging changes the attack behavior. Megiddo 300 21 Dark attribute technique. Fires a dark photon bullet in front of you. Charging changes the attack behavior. Gi Megiddo 320 22 Dark attribute technique. Generates a dark photon implosion in front of you. Charging changes the attack behavior. Composite Attribute Fomergion - - A composite attribute technique of fire and darkness. Activates by consuming the composite attribute technique gauge. Generates a huge sword of flame and darkness and mowing down the front. Berlanzion - - Combined attribute technique of ice and light. Activates by consuming the composite attribute technique gauge. Generates and throws a spear of ice and light, creating an ice vortex in front of you. Zandion - - A combined attribute technique of lightning and wind. Activates by consuming the composite attribute technique gauge. After charging and dancing in lightning and wind, it creates a tornado and retreats. ▼ Change history 2023/06/07 Power upward revision Foie 255→300 Guy Foie 410→430 Bertha 280→300 Guy Bertha 250→310 Guy Sonde 320→330 Zan 260→290 Guy Zan 250→270 Granz 290→310 Gi Grants 240→270 Megiddo 280→300 Gi Megiddo 310→320 2022/04/06 Addition of complex attribute techniques 2021/12/15 Addition of wind/light/dark attribute techniques 2021/10/13 Upward correction of power Foie 240→255 Guy Foie 380→410 Bata 250→280 Gi Bata 225→250 Sonde 260→300 Gi Sonde 270→320 ▲ ▼ Comment Displaying the latest 20 items. === [Tasks] ===
Task HTML ConvertTime 0.218 sec. Table of Contents Overview Guide marker How to check completed tasks Task list Main Side Daily Weekly Limited time Limited time tasks currently being distributed "PSO2 Day" limited time tasks Creative space related limited time tasks Limited time tasks that have ended distribution Small details on weapon specification tasks Comments Overview Main menu → Tasks / Quests → Tasks, or Simple status You can check the task list by pressing the [R] key or the X button while the is displayed. By meeting the achievement conditions, you can advance the story, unlock features, and receive rewards. The effect of completing a task will occur automatically upon completion, and you will receive the reward on the spot. Therefore, it is not possible to achieve the goal and receive the reward using a different character. It corresponds to the old PSO2's Client Order and Arks Mission. Main Task An important task that serves as a substitute for story progression or a tutorial. It is a condition for unlocking some sections and high-rank sections. Side Task A task from an NPC other than the main task. There are a wide variety of activities such as defeating designated enemies, collecting them, and exploring them, and many of them reward you with a large amount of experience points. Daily/Weekly Tasks Tasks that are not accepted from a specific NPC, but are automatically accepted when you log in. In order to receive an order, it is necessary to make some progress in the main task. Immediate completion reward. Be careful of ships and characters when receiving rewards. Limited time task A task that occurs only during an event, etc. for a limited time. Please note that there is a set period in which you can accept orders, and you will no longer be able to receive or report orders after that period. ▼ Change history 2023/01/25 Update In the task guide display, the specifications have been changed so that limited-time tasks and daily tasks are displayed on the guide with priority. ▲ ▼ Guide Marker Tasks have a guide marker function that indicates the location of the target point, so even beginners can get to the target point without getting lost. If you accept multiple tasks, the guide marker for the last task you accepted will be automatically displayed, but you can change the task for which the guide marker will be displayed from the task list displayed by selecting ``Tasks/Quests'' from the menu. ▲ ▼ How to check completed tasks You can check by changing "In progress" to "Completed" at the top of the task list. ▲ ▼ Task list ▲ ▼ Main See main story. ▲ ▼ Click on the side request NPC to go to the task details for each NPC. For some tasks, such as Trainier's Conqueror, if you accept the task after fulfilling the conditions, you can hear special lines. The maximum number of unfulfilled orders is 20, which increases to 40 if you use the premium set. Request NPC Features Common to all regions Riamay Tasks that can be ordered every day, some weekly tasks Ronaldin Loop tasks for advanced users Tim Team tasks For limited tasks that can be accepted only once, refer to each NPC in NPCs. ▲ ▼ Daily *Only once per day per account. Unlocked by completing ``Talk to Aina at the Rest Corner'' in the main task ``Preparing for Battle.'' A task that can be ordered once a day. A total of five tasks will be selected, one from each genre. The content changes will occur every day at 4am, similar to the Gathering. Although it can be said that orders are almost automatically accepted, you will need to "log in," "move to a room," or "view the task list." In other words, if you cross 4 o'clock while playing, you will need to consciously re-order. If you forget to order a task, you may fail to complete the daily task after the content has been updated. (For example, when you realize that you forgot to update after completing a section-specified gathering, etc.) Players who are active during this time should get into the habit of opening the task list. In the opposite case, if you have tasks left for the previous day, if you can accomplish them in that room, you can complete them and receive the reward before retaking the order. You can also use this method to follow up on cases where you failed to achieve the goal due to forgetting to update, as mentioned earlier. (You need to wake up around 4 o'clock...) The contents of the task will change depending on the progress of the main task, and the task corresponding to the character whose main task is the most progressed in the account will be issued. If you proceed with a low-level character after high-level tasks have been issued, be aware that while you will gain a lot of experience points if you complete them, there are also tasks that will become virtually impossible to progress. Guide marker function is also effective. Gathering materials and Tame's Enemies will also guide you, so make use of them. ▼ Change history 2026/06/03 "Clear Luciel Exploration" replaced with "Use Team Mag" 2024/04/15 Monday & Tuesday only! Unlocked by completing ``Talk to Aina at the Rest Corner'' in the main task ``Preparing for Battle.'' A task that can be ordered once a week. The week changes from the end of maintenance every Wednesday until the start of maintenance the following Wednesday. If there is no maintenance, it will switch to Wednesday at 12:00. Please note that due to the timing of daily tasks and order acceptance, if you proceed before the update, the "daily task achievement" for that day may not be counted. Conversely, it is now possible to make up for last week's unachieved goals even on Wednesday. ▼ Change history 2024/09/04 Changed "Defeat Dark Falz Souras" to "Defeat Dark Falz Dallion (Rank.2)" 2024/06/19 Changed "Defeat Dark Falz Dallion" to "Defeat Dark Falz Souras (Rank.2)" 2024/04/17 "Defeat Dark Falz Souras" changed to "Defeat Dark Falz Dallion" 2023/10/11 Adjustment of weekly tasks Relaxation of "Emergency Quest Clearance" achievement condition from 3 times to [1 time] Added new task "Daily Task Achievement" (Achievement condition: 10 times) Added "Special Scratch Ticket x1" to the reward for the same task (25 times condition). === [Urgent Quests] ===
Urgent Quest HTML ConvertTime 0.189 sec. Table of Contents Overview List of Urgent Quests Limited time How to order Proposal to start the quest immediately If the ordering time ends during matching Password matching Matching in some specific blocks such as recommended photography Incapacity to fight during emergency quest and Resta sign/Reverser sign Laws of occurrence of emergency quest Down gauge Rewards for defeating enemies and temporary quest storage Victory effect Comments Overview A special quest that occurs randomly and can only be accepted for 30 minutes from the time it occurs. Battles occur in specific locations in the exploration section, but battles take place in an isolated state like a dedicated room. Order conditions are set for each quest. When they occur, they occur simultaneously in each region. The number of times that quests that occur at the same time can be cleared is the same. Even if the quest is from a different region, you cannot accept it more than the number of times you can complete it. It has been announced that the announced emergency quests that existed in the old PSO2 will not be available except in special cases such as when events are held. ▼ Addition/Change History 2025/11/05 Addition of Residue of Ambition Rank.3, Addition of Star Crushing Rush Rank.2, 2025/07/02 Resource Extraction Rig Defense Battle: Addition of Rithem Rank.4, Addition of Sign of Star Extinction Rank.2 2025/02/05 PSO2es' "Notification just before the occurrence of an emergency quest" and "Notification 1 hour before the occurrence of an emergency quest" are now compatible with NGS. 2024/10/02 In the emergency quest, the specifications have been changed to occur once every 2 to 3 hours. 1 Combat power 3413 or higher 75 8 people Operation Happy Wrap (2023/4/19-6/7) West Airio Rakau Coast - Combat power 3119 or higher 70 8 people Senhen Banka's Enemy Clearance Battle (2022/3/9-4/6) West Aerio Cryano Cliffs 1 Combat power 1184 or higher 17 8 people 2 Combat power 1898 or higher 40 ▲ ▼ How to order A pre-start warning announcement will be played in the game 15 minutes before the emergency quest occurs, and an icon will be displayed on the world map and at the location where it occurred. When an emergency quest occurs, you will be able to accept it. If you are in the same section as the place where the quest occurred, a message to accept the quest will be displayed on the screen. (Please note that if you have your sword drawn or an access command like "[E] Take" is displayed, the guide will not be displayed.) Even if you are not in the same section as the place where the quest occurred, you can accept the quest by selecting the icon on the world map. Once you accept the order, it will become "Matching". 人数が揃うと自動的に専用の隔離されたフィールドに転送される マッチング中にセクションを跨ぐとマッチングがキャンセルされるので注意 複数人がマッチングしている状態でテレポート・セクションを跨ぐなどの形でマッチングをキャンセルすると、その回の緊急クエストに参加できなくなる（クイック受注不可になり、マップ上の緊急クエストのアイコンも全て消えてしまう） 1分弱のカウントダウンでクエストが開始される 転送中もカウントダウンされているためカウントダウンの時間は前後する ▲ ▼ クエスト即時開始の提案 マッチング中に「クエスト受注メニュー」から「クエスト即時開始の提案」を行うと、参加中のメンバーの同意が得られれば、人数が揃わなくてもクエストを開始できる。 多数決ではなくメンバー全員の同意がなければ即時開始はされず、反対が一人でもいれば提案は却下される。 無回答は提案時間終了と同時に同意扱いとなり、即時開始される。 数回即時開始提案を拒否すると拒否者をマッチングから除外した上で同意者のみで即時開始が可決される。 提案中も新規参加者は受付状態であり、他に定員に達するだけの人数が待機状態になった時点で合流処理が入り、開始となる。 クエストによるが、緊急クエストはエネミーHPに参加人数補正がかかるため、少人数でもクリア失敗しにくくなっている。 ▲ ▼ マッチング中に受注時間が終了した場合 マッチングは維持したまま、クエスト開始確認の案内が届き一定時間後にクエストが開始される。 新着通知から即時開始の同意をすることで、待ち時間なくクエストを開始することができる。 ▲ ▼ パスワードマッチング 参加パスワードを設定して「他パーティーとのマルチプレイも制限」を選択すると、同じ参加パスワードを設定しているプレイヤーとのみマッチングする。 固定パーティーでプレイしたい場合などに利用できる。 「a」のような単純なパスワードを設定すると、偶然同じ参加パスワードを設定しているプレイヤーともマッチングしてしまうため注意。 *1 ▲ ▼ 撮影推奨など一部特定ブロックでのマッチング 撮影推奨ブロック、ビギナー推奨ブロック、交流推奨ブロックでクエストを受注した場合は、同じ種類のブロック間でのみマッチングが行われる。 ビギナー推奨ブロックでは、エアリオリージョン以外のクエストの受注、マッチングができない。 *2 これらのブロックはブロック数が少なくプレイヤーも少ないため、マッチングに時間がかかったり、マッチング出来ない場合がある。 現在のブロック名はメインメニューを開いた際の「簡易ステータス」の上に表示される。 ▲ ▼ 緊急クエスト中の戦闘不能とレスタサイン・リバーサーサイン 緊急クエスト中に戦闘不能になった場合、一定時間後に自動的にその場で復活する。 リバーサーサインが使われた場合は通常と同様にすぐに復活する。 緊急クエストのフィールドに配置されているレスタサイン・リバーサーサインは、クエスト中に再配置されない。 ▲ ▼ 緊急クエストの発生法則 2024/10/02アップデート以降、2～3時間に1回は必ず発生するように仕様が変更された。 ※2025/02/05アップデートにて、PSOesの緊急予告機能がNGSに対応した。プレミアム期間中は1時間前に発生予告が送られてくる。 ※緊急クエストの予告・発生履歴について、当Wikiでは 緊急掲示板 で情報交換されている。 その他、XなどのSNS、Discordなどのチャットツールにおいて有志が情報提供していることがある。 以下は過去の発生法則についての記述。 ▼ 過去の仕様 発生確率に関する出典: NGS検証データ置場 緊急クエストの発生は前回の発生時から2～4時間後。 それぞれ以下の確率となっている。 2時間後 25% 3時間後 50% 4時間後 25% ステージライブ が発生した場合はライブ発生から30分後に緊急クエストが発生するため、0分からではなく30分からになる。 ▲ ▼ ダウンゲージ 与ダメージ補正と蓄積に関する出典: NGS検証データ置場 討伐戦タイプの緊急クエストでは、画面上部中央にエネミーのHPゲージと、その下に「ダウンゲージ」が表示される。 ダウンゲージはエネミーを攻撃することで減少していき、ゼロになると「BREAK」の表示と砕け散るような紫色のエフェクトとともに、エネミーが一定時間ダウンする。 *3 BREAKによるダウン中はプレイヤーが与えるダメージに 2倍 の補正がかかる。 ダメージ2倍補正はBREAKダウン時に付与される 特殊デバフが消滅するまで有効。 ダウン中はダウンゲージが徐々に増加し、完全に回復するとダウン状態から復帰する。 ゲージ回復中でも、内部的にはすでにゲージは全快しており、 Gauge reduction by attacks while down is effective. Although it is a performance that reduces the gauge, it seems that the mechanism is that it occurs by accumulating physical down value and attribute down value.The amount of gauge decrease increases as the physical down value and attribute down value increase with Bouncer's class skill Defeat Amplify.In addition to normal attacks, down gauge is also reduced by physical down, attribute down, and special down by destroying some parts.If the down gauge becomes zero during physical down/attribute down, it will be down again by BREAK after recovering from physical down/attribute down. Defeat class skills related to Bouncer's "downed enemy" are not applied when downed by BREAK.See also Enemy Down/Broken Parts for various types of downed. ▲ ▼ Rewards for defeating enemies and temporary storage for quests In emergency quests, even if you defeat an enemy, "GREAT!!" will be displayed and you will not be able to obtain experience points, meseta, or drop items*4 on the spot. Instead, you will be able to obtain them as a clear reward immediately after clearing the quest. *5 After completing the results, the clear reward ``Receive Reward Items'' will be displayed, and you will be able to individually select whether to transfer or sell item packs or other warehouses. The automatic item sale function does not apply to items stored as clear rewards, but all items that are not received will be sold, so you only need to obtain what you need. If you accidentally sell something you need, you can buy it back at the item shop before logging out. In addition, the drop items as clear rewards are stored in the "Quest Temporary Warehouse" when you clear the emergency quest. If you return to the ARCS base without paying for any reason *6, you can open the receipt screen from the new notification on the main menu. It can also be retrieved from the regular warehouse menu. The contents of the "Quest Temporary Storage" will be saved even if you log out. However, you will not be able to accept new quests if any items remain in the "quest temporary storage". ▲ ▼ Victory Effect Certain emergency quests have a victory effect where the quest participants line up and take a victory pose when the quest is cleared. For the victory pose, you can set lobby actions (only some) and facial expressions from the main menu → chat → victory effect settings.In addition, the character names of yourself and your party members and the title set on the ARCS card will be displayed. (Unlike the old PSO2, cleared quest information etc. is not displayed.) ▲ ▼ Comments Displaying the latest 20. Success rate increase period Comments Overview Special abilities can be added to weapons and armor using ``Add Special Abilities'' in the item labs in Central City, Ritem City, Kvaris Camp, and Stear Camp. Adding special abilities requires special ability capsules and N-Meseta. Special ability capsules can be obtained from enemy drops, special ability capsule exchange, treasure shops, etc. The types of special ability capsules that drop differ depending on the section and enemy type. See Details for more information. Preset abilities, which are given to dropped weapons and armor with a low probability, are different from special abilities. Dasha Diable I~III Added Dru's Sole III 2021/07/07 Added Thunder Weaker I ▲ ▼ Additional success rate Special ability capsules have an additional success rate set for each type. You can increase your success rate by consuming up to 10 capsules of the same type at the same time. For example, the additional success rate of Power I is 10%, so if you use 10 of them, it becomes 100%. You can also increase your success rate with auxiliary items. For additional success rates for each type of special ability, see the ``Success Rate'' column in the Special Abilities List. S Capsule Some special ability capsules have an "S" at the end. The rarity has been changed from the original capsule, and its special ability is treated as a different type from the regular product without S. This is mainly relevant when referring to the number of types of special abilities, such as the latent ability ``Skill Type'' or the preset ability ``Fixer Abundance''. This capsule has a 100% success rate and can always grant special abilities with just one consumption. It can be obtained from Treasure Shop SG, limited time SG Shop, specific AC scratch bonuses, etc. ▲ ▼ Number of special abilities that can be added (number of slots) The number of special abilities that can be added (number of slots) is determined by the reinforcement value of the equipment. For details on strengthening equipment, see Item Enhancement/Limit Breaking. Enhancement value Number of slots Unenhanced ~ +19 2 +20 ~ +39 3 +40 ~ +49 4 +50 ~ +60 5 +61 ~ +80 6 +81 ~ +99 7 +100 8 ▲ ▼ Effect of special ability HP Maximum HP increases (decreases if negative) PP Maximum PP increases Power [%] Increases the power of all types of weapons Strike weapon power [%] Increases the power of strike weapons Shooting weapon power [%] Increases the power of shooting weapons Legal weapon power [%] Increases the power of legal weapons Lower power limit correction [%] Corrects the lower limit of the weapon's "damage correction" (variation range of damage dealt to enemies) Damage resistance [%] Damage received decreases (increases if negative) Status Abnormality Resistance [%] Makes it harder to get status abnormalities (easier if negative) Special Environmental Resistance [%] Reduces environmental damage that occurs in a specific section The effects of powerful special abilities are multiplied. For example, if a total of 4 parts (weapon and 3 parts of armor) have Power III (2.0%) attached, it will be 1.02 x 1.02 x 1.02 x 1.02 = approximately 1.082, and the power will increase by approximately 8.2%. See also damage calculation. Special environmental resistance effects are added. For example, if 3 parts of armor have Decord Standard III (25.0%), 0.25 + 0.25 + 0.25 = 0.75, and environmental damage will be reduced by 75%. ▲ ▼ Inheritance of special abilities Once added, special abilities will not be lost unless they are overwritten. The inheritance success rate is always 100%. Therefore, it is not necessary to succeed in all additions at once, and it is possible to add multiple times. Since the equipment itself cannot be used as a material, special abilities cannot be transferred to other equipment. ▲ ▼ Special ability transplant Implemented on June 5, 2024 Related > Announcement "Special ability transplant & N-Special ability transplant pass (Players site) Features available in the item lab. By consuming N-Special ability transplant pass x1 and 10,000 N-Meseta, it is possible to transplant special abilities (all or EX special abilities only). N-Special ability transplant pass is item recycling You can exchange it for 100 N-Special Abilities Transplant Pass Supplementary Tickets that can be obtained from title rewards, etc. ① Transplanting all Special Abilities If the number of special ability slots in the base item and material item is the same, or if the base item has more, it can be transferred regardless of the series or weapon type. All special abilities of the base item will disappear and be replaced with the special abilities of the material item. If the number of special abilities of the material item is less than the slot of the base item, all remaining slots of the base item will be empty. All special abilities of the material item will disappear and become empty slots. ② Transplanting EX special abilities Only if the number of EX special abilities of the base item and material item are the same, the normal special abilities will remain for both the base item and the material item. All EX special abilities of the base item will disappear and be replaced by the EX special abilities of the material item.If you want to attach an EX special ability to a weapon that does not have an EX special ability, you will need to port it first.Due to the specifications of ①, if the weapon you want to attach an EX special ability to has any ability, it is strongly recommended that you port it first. With the implementation of EX special ability A in the form of a capsule on January 1, 2026, it has become possible to retrofit EX frames with a complicated procedure and additional costs.For details, see the specifications for EX special ability A.Weapons can only be transferred between weapons and armor.Currently equipped items cannot be selected and transferred when selecting a material item, and all the special abilities that have been granted. There are weapon series that can be transplanted without consuming the N-Special Abilities Transplant Pass.Transplanting using these as material items (transfer source) does not consume the pass (Meseta is consumed).It supports both "transplanting all special abilities" and "transplanting EX special abilities". The base item (transfer destination) can be anything. On the other hand, even if you select these weapons as a base, if the materials are not compatible, the porting pass will be consumed as usual, so be careful. Weapon series that do not require a pass for porting are listed below. ★11 ``Eredimu'' series ★12 ``Claudim'' series ★13 ``Hubert'' series ★13 ``Vorheur'' series ★13 ``Eindale'' series ★14 ``Spradio'' series Currently, compatible weapons have one thing in common: ``EX special abilities are initially given at the time of drop.'' ▲ ▼ Auxiliary items These auxiliary items are consumed regardless of the success or failure of adding special abilities. Rare item text How to obtain 5 N-Special ability addition success rate +5% Increases the probability of successful addition when adding special abilities to an item. Old login bonus 5 N-Special ability addition success rate +10% Login bonus Item exchange Mission pass (gold) 5 N-Special ability addition success rate +15% Item recycling Special scratch 9 N-Special ability addition success rate +20% AC scratch number bonus SG scratch number bonus Special scratch Treasure shop (premium) 9 N-Special ability addition success rate +30% AC scratch number bonus SG scratch number bonus 9 N-Special ability addition success rate +40% AC scratch number bonus SG scratch number bonus 5 N-Special ability addition item protection Prevents material items from being consumed when adding special ability to an item fails. Item Recycling Treasure Shop (Premium) AC Scratch Number of Times Bonus SG Scratch Number of Times Bonus SG Shop Specifications of ``N-Special ability addition item protection'' As the item description (text) indicates, when special ability addition fails, only the ``material item'', that is, the special ability capsule, will no longer be consumed. Auxiliary items (N-Special ability addition item protection, N-Special ability addition success rate +◯%) and costs (N-Meseta) are consumed. If you add multiple special abilities at the same time, it will be considered a failure unless all of them are successful, so if you want to use valuable capsules, it is better to add them individually. Although it is not mentioned in the explanation, it also prevents the disappearance of special abilities that have been granted in the event that replacement and addition fail. (Example: If replacing Al's Secrete III → Ability IV fails, Al's Secrete III will remain as is.) ▲ ▼ List of special abilities Abilities of the same category or with the same name such as Stamina I and Stamina II will conflict, and only one can be added to one piece of equipment. Abilities with category "-" do not conflict. For a table that allows you to sort effects, see Special Abilities/List Comparison Table, and for special abilities of equipment brought in from old PSO2, see Special Abilities/Old PSO2 Version. (P.01 and P.02, etc. of the same system, such as Elisis, conflict.) The effect will only be applied when the target Duel Quest is in progress and a weapon that can be equipped by the main class is used. The effect will be applied even if added to armor, but the effect will only be applied when a main class weapon is equipped. In addition, when using multiple weapons, if one is a main class weapon and the other is a different class weapon, the effect will only be applied when the main class weapon is equipped. (*Example: If a hunter is equipped with a multi-weapon of a sword and an assault rifle, the effect will only be applied when used as a sword.) As an aside, the name is thought to be derived from the solar system's dwarf planets or dwarf planet candidates: Pluto, Makemake, Haumea, Eris, Ceres, and Sedna. A special ability that provides an effect. *From "Season Winterfeve '23" onwards, there is an effect of +3% power on other than seasonal enemies.It is always given to specific weapons dropped during the seasonal event period. It will not be awarded if dropped outside of the seasonal event period. There are no capsule drops. The (apparent) success rate increases if you use them all at once, but by using one capsule at a time, you can add ``N-ability additional success rate + α%'' each time. In conclusion, if you use success rate increasing items and insert capsules one by one, the expected value per capsule will increase, but the probability of not being successful at all increases. In other words, if you repeat inserting one capsule 10 times, you will succeed two or more times = the possibility of making multiple equipment with that ability will increase (If you try 18% success rate 10 times by combining one capsule with an 8% success rate and a 10% success rate, the expected value is that you will complete 1.8 pieces of equipment with that ability, but there is a ``probability of not succeeding at all'' of about 13.74%). 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82 x 0.82≒0.1374 Conversely, if you put in 10 items at once, the probability of success increases, but no matter how you try, you will only complete one piece of equipment with that ability. (If you use 10 capsules with an 8% success rate and a 10% success rate to achieve a 90% success rate once, the expected value is to complete 0.9 pieces of equipment with that ability, but the probability of failure is suppressed to 10%.) Consult with the inventory of capsules and items that increase success rate, and if you want to prepare multiple units such as one item, Try to find a way to use each weapon that is good for your mental health, such as putting 10 of them in a weapon you absolutely want to use, or finding a way to get a success rate of 30 to 80% with 2 to 9. ▲ ▼ Success rate increase period Reward period Table of Contents Overview ARCS History: Main Story Prologue Chapter 1 Chapter 2 Chapter 3 Chapter 4 Chapter 5 Chapter 6 Chapter 7 Chapter 8 ARKS History: Others Short Episodes Aina Manon Bruder Crawford Ran Naderev Hardy Meri Irma Glenn Kanui Others Comments Overview Implemented on June 8, 2022 You can re-order cleared story quests, side stories, and play viewed event scenes. You can accept orders from the quest counters in Central City, Ritem City, Kvaris Camp, and Stear Camp, or from the main menu → Tasks/Quests → Quest Counter. STORY Story Quest A battle occurs. Clear rewards and drop items cannot be obtained. SIDE Side Story A battle occurs. The required combat power is set. Clear rewards and drop items cannot be obtained. SCENE Scene playback Only event scenes, no battles occur. ▼ Guiden's line when logging in for the first time after implementing the quest counter I heard from Ryurin that a new “quest counter” has been established. At the quest counter, you can relive what happened in the past through the data of each mug. In addition, it seems that you can also do battle simulations based on records of past battles. There may be some aspects that feel different from actual combat, but there are things you can learn from them. Seeing is believing. Go to the quest counter with the class you want to gain experience with. Looks like you've arrived. Your past experiences are recorded as "Arks History". It seems that more battle simulation training menus will be added from time to time. Please check it regularly.
